| MCP:umysWT | # SpringBoot Java Template | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url?style=social&url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fgeminikims)](https://twitter.com/geminikims) | MCP:umysWT | [![Youtube](https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/views/UCDh8zEDofOcrOMAOnSVL9Tg?label=Youtube&style=social)](https://www.youtube.com/@geminikims) | MCP:umysWT | [![CI](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml) | MCP:umysWT | [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | This is not the best structure. This is a good basic structure to use early in the project when productivity is important. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | Remember, as your software grows, your structure must grow too. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | # **Modules** | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## Core | MCP:umysWT | Each submodule of this module is responsible for one domain service. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | This must make the modular structure grow with the growth of the service. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ### core:core-api | MCP:umysWT | It is the only executable module in the project. It is structured to have domains to maximize initial development productivity. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | It is also responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks for services. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ### core:core-enum | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | This module contains enums that are used by `core-api` and must be delivered to external modules. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## Clients | MCP:umysWT | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with external systems. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ### clients:clients-example | MCP:umysWT | This module shows an example of HTTP communication with `Spring-Cloud-Open-Feign`. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## Storage | MCP:umysWT | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with the various storages. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ### storage:db-core | MCP:umysWT | This module shows an example of connecting to `MySql` using `Spring-Data-JPA`. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## Support | MCP:umysWT | Submodules of this module are responsible for additional support. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ### support:logging | MCP:umysWT | This module supports logging of service and has a dependency added for distributed tracing support. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | It also includes dependencies to support `Sentry`. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ### support:monitoring | MCP:umysWT | This module supports monitoring of services. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## Tests | MCP:umysWT | Submodules of this module are responsible for the convenience of writing test codes. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ### tests:api-docs | MCP:umysWT | This module is for writing spring-rest-docs conveniently. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | # Dependency Management | MCP:umysWT | All dependency versioning is done through `gradle.properties` file. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | If you want to add a new dependency, put the version in `gradle.properties` and load it in `build.gradle`. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | # Runtime Profiles | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## local | MCP:umysWT | This profile aims to configure an environment that can be developed even if the network is disconnected. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## local-dev | MCP:umysWT | This profile aims configurations that allow me to connect to the DEV environment from my local machine. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## dev | MCP:umysWT | This profile exists for deploying Development environments. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## staging | MCP:umysWT | This profile exists for deploying Staging environments. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## live | MCP:umysWT | This profile exists for deploying Live environments. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | # Test Tasks & Tags | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## test | MCP:umysWT | This is a collection of test-tasks that we want to run on `CI`. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | If you want to change the settings, modify the `build.gradle` file. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## unitTest | MCP:umysWT | This is a group of tests that typically have no dependencies, are fast to run, and test a single feature. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## contextTest | MCP:umysWT | This is a task that runs with SpringContext and has integration tests. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## restDocsTest | MCP:umysWT | This is a task to create asciidoc based on spring-rest-docs. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## developTest | MCP:umysWT | This is a task of tests that should not be run in `CI`. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | This is a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT |
| MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | # Recommended Preferences | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## Git Hook | MCP:umysWT | This setting makes run `lint` on every commit. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ``` | MCP:umysWT | $ git config core.hookspath .githooks | MCP:umysWT | ``` | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ## IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:umysWT | This setting makes it easier to run the `test code` out of the box. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | ``` | MCP:umysWT | // Gradle Build and run with IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:umysWT | Build, Execution, Deployment > Build Tools > Gradle > Run tests using > IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:umysWT | ``` | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | If you want to apply lint settings to the format of IDEA, please refer to the guide below. | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea) | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | --- | MCP:umysWT | | MCP:umysWT | # Supported By | MCP:umysWT |

data.content:
Now I have a complete picture of the repository. Here's the summary:

---

## Module Structure of `spring-boot-java-template`

This is a **multi-module Gradle Spring Boot project** (`spring-boot-java-template`) organized into **5 top-level module groups**, each containing one or more submodules. The architecture follows a clean separation-of-concerns pattern designed for early-stage projects where development productivity matters.

### 📁 Project Layout

```
spring-boot-java-template/
├── core/
│   ├── core-api        (executable application)
│   └── core-enum       (shared enumerations)
├── clients/
│   └── client-example  (external HTTP integrations)
├── storage/
│   └── db-core         (database/persistence layer)
├── support/
│   ├── logging         (tracing & error reporting)
│   └── monitoring      (metrics & health checks)
└── tests/
    └── api-docs        (REST API documentation tests)
```

---

### 1. **`core/`** — Domain & Application Layer

This is the heart of the application. Each submodule is meant to handle one domain service, allowing the structure to scale as the service grows.

| Submodule | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`core-api`** | The **only executable module** (the one that produces a boot JAR). It wires together all other modules, exposes REST APIs via Spring WebMVC, and bootstraps the Spring Boot application. Depends on every other module. |
| **`core-enum`** | Contains **shared enumerations** used across `core-api` and any external modules that need them. Has no dependencies — a pure, stable contract module. |

---

### 2. **`clients/`** — External System Integrations

Submodules here are responsible for communicating with **external (third-party) services**.

| Submodule | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`client-example`** | Demonstrates HTTP client integration using **Spring Cloud OpenFeign** with HC5 (Apache HttpClient 5) and Feign-Micrometer for observability. A template for building declarative REST clients. |

---

### 3. **`storage/`** — Data Persistence Layer

Submodules handle integration with **data storage systems**.

| Submodule | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`db-core`** | Shows database access using **Spring Data JPA**. Configured for **MySQL** (production) with an **H2** in-memory database available for local/test use. Depends on `core-enum` for entity-related enumerations. |

---

### 4. **`support/`** — Cross-Cutting Infrastructure Concerns

Submodules provide **operational support** — things the application needs at runtime but that aren't part of business logic.

| Submodule | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`logging`** | Sets up **structured logging** with distributed tracing support via **Spring Boot OpenTelemetry** and error reporting via **Sentry** (Logback integration). |
| **`monitoring`** | Provides **health checks and metrics** via **Spring Boot Actuator** with a **Prometheus** metrics endpoint for scraping. |

---

### 5. **`tests/`** — Test Infrastructure & Documentation

Submodules support **testing convenience** and test-generated artifacts.

| Submodule | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`api-docs`** | Provides **Spring REST Docs** test support (MockMvc-based) for auto-generating AsciiDoc API documentation. Used as a `testImplementation` dependency by `core-api`. |

---

### 🔗 Dependency Graph

The `core-api` module sits at the top of the dependency tree, pulling in everything:

```
core-api
├── core-enum
├── storage/db-core  →  core-enum
├── clients/client-example
├── support/logging
├── support/monitoring
└── tests/api-docs  (test only)
```

### 🧪 Testing Strategy

The project defines **four tagged test categories** in the root `build.gradle`:
- **`unitTest`** — Fast, isolated tests (excludes `develop`, `context`, `restdocs` tags)
- **`contextTest`** — Spring context integration tests
- **`restDocsTest`** — Spring REST Docs tests (generates AsciiDoc)
- **`developTest`** — Development-only tests excluded from CI

### 🌐 Runtime Profiles

Five Spring profiles are supported: `local` (offline dev), `local-dev` (local connecting to DEV), `dev`, `staging`, and `live`.

---

**In essence**, this template provides a well-organized, production-ready Spring Boot scaffold that cleanly separates business logic (`core`), external integrations (`clients`), persistence (`support`), operational concerns (`support`), and test tooling (`tests`) — giving teams a solid foundation to build upon as the project grows.
